<!-- Removes the Flash of Unstyled Content when in dark mode. -->
<script>
  (function () {
    let isDarkMode = false;
    let savedMode;

    try {
      savedMode = localStorage.getItem('FatCalc-IsDarkMode');
      if (savedMode) {
        isDarkMode = JSON.parse(savedMode);
      } else {
        isDarkMode =
          window.matchMedia &&
          window.matchMedia('(prefers-color-scheme: dark)').matches;
      }

      window['fatcalc-app'] = window['fatcalc-app'] || {};
      window['fatcalc-app'].isDarkMode = isDarkMode;

      const scheme = isDarkMode ? 'dark' : 'light';
      document.body.style.setProperty('color-scheme', scheme);
    } catch (e) {
      console.error("FatCalc: ", e);
    }
  })();
</script>

This approach ensures larger individuals receive appropriately higher recommendations. The calculator converts between imperial and metric units automatically.</p><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Activity Level</h3><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Physical activity significantly increases protein requirements. Here's how different activity levels affect your needs:</p><div _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="formula-box"><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Sedentary:</b> 0.8–1.0 g/kg — Minimal physical activity<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Lightly Active:</b> 1.0–1.2 g/kg — Light exercise 1–3 days/week<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Moderately Active:</b> 1.2–1.4 g/kg — Moderate exercise 3–5 days/week<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Active:</b> 1.4–1.6 g/kg — Hard exercise 6–7 days/week<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Very Active:</b> 1.6–1.8 g/kg — Very hard exercise, physical job<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Athlete:</b> 1.8–2.2 g/kg — Professional/competitive training </div><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Fitness Goals</h3><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Your specific goals modify the base protein recommendation:</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Maintain Weight:</b> Uses the baseline recommendation for your activity level. Adequate protein helps preserve lean mass during weight maintenance.</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Build Muscle:</b> Adds approximately 0.2 g/kg to the recommendation. Research consistently shows that higher protein intake supports muscle protein synthesis during resistance training.</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Lose Weight:</b> Adds approximately 0.3 g/kg to the recommendation. Higher protein intake during caloric restriction helps preserve muscle mass and increases satiety.</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Endurance Training:</b> Adds approximately 0.1 g/kg. Endurance athletes have elevated protein needs for muscle repair and adaptation, though typically less than strength athletes.</p><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Age Considerations</h3><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Adults over 65 have increased protein requirements due to anabolic resistance, a reduced ability to use dietary protein for muscle synthesis. The calculator automatically adjusts recommendations upward for older adults, with a minimum of 1.0–1.2 g/kg regardless of activity level.</p><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Pregnancy and Breastfeeding</h3><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Pregnant and breastfeeding individuals require additional protein to support fetal development and milk production. The calculator adds approximately 25g per day for each condition, based on current nutrition guidelines.</p><h2 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">The RDA vs. Optimal Protein Intake</h2><div _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="note-box"><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Important:</b> The Recommended Dietary Allowance (RDA) of 0.8 g/kg represents the minimum to prevent deficiency in sedentary individuals. It is not an optimal target for active people or those with specific fitness goals. </div><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Research over the past two decades has consistently shown that protein intakes well above the RDA provide benefits for active individuals. The International Society of Sports Nutrition, the American College of Sports Medicine, and numerous meta-analyses support protein intakes of 1.4–2.0 g/kg for exercising individuals.</p><h2 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Protein Distribution Throughout the Day</h2><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">How you distribute your protein intake matters for muscle protein synthesis. Research suggests that consuming 20–40 grams of high-quality protein per meal optimally stimulates muscle protein synthesis. The calculator provides a per-meal target based on dividing your daily goal across three main meals.</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Key principles for protein timing:</p><div _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="formula-box"><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Even Distribution:</b> Spread protein intake across 3–5 meals rather than concentrating in one meal<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Post-Exercise:</b> Consume protein within a few hours after training to support recovery<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Before Sleep:</b> A protein-rich snack before bed may support overnight muscle protein synthesis<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Quality Matters:</b> Complete protein sources with all essential amino acids are most effective </div><h2 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Protein Sources</h2><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Complete proteins contain all essential amino acids in adequate amounts. Animal sources like meat, fish, eggs, and dairy are complete proteins. Plant proteins can be combined throughout the day to provide all essential amino acids.</p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">High-quality protein sources per 100g serving:</p><div _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="formula-box"><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Chicken Breast:</b> ~31g protein<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Salmon:</b> ~25g protein<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Greek Yogurt:</b> ~10g protein<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Eggs:</b> ~13g protein (2 large eggs)<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Tofu:</b> ~8g protein<br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><br _ngcontent-ng-c127155920=""><b _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Lentils (cooked):</b> ~9g protein </div><h2 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">When to Consult a Professional</h2><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="note-box"><span _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">This calculator provides general guidance based on published research. Individuals with kidney disease, certain metabolic conditions, or specific medical needs should consult a registered dietitian or physician before significantly changing protein intake.</span></p><p _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">A sports dietitian can provide personalized recommendations based on your specific training program, body composition goals, and dietary preferences.</p><app-related-stuff _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" _nghost-ng-c206835557="" ngh="1"><div _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" class="medium-body"><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="">Related Calculators:</h3><ul _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><li _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><a _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" href="/macro">Macro Calculator</a></li><li _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><a _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" href="/mm">Muscle Mass Calculator</a></li><li _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><a _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" href="/body-recomp-calculator">Body Recomposition Calculator</a></li><li _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><a _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" href="/one-rep-max-calculator">One Rep Max (1RM) Calculator</a></li><li _ngcontent-ng-c206835557=""><a _ngcontent-ng-c206835557="" href="/creatine-calculator">Creatine Dosage Calculator</a></li></ul></div></app-related-stuff><h3 _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">References:</h3><ol _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="" class="citation-list"><li _ngcontent-ng-c127155920="">Jäger R, Kerksick CM, Campbell BI, et al.
